    Animate the display to count up (or down) to the current target value.

    add(int) - Method in class Counter
    Add a new score to the current counter value.
    addedToWorld(World) - Method in class Ifv

    addedToWorld(World) - Method in class SuperStatBar
     
    addedToWorld(World) - Method in class Tank
     
    addedToWorld(World) - Method in class Vehicle
    This method is called automatically when the Vehicle is added to the World, and places the Vehicle just off screen (centered 100 pixels beyond the center of the lane spawner) so it will appear to roll onto the screen smoothly.
    addedToWorld(World) - Method in class Windstorm

    getImageRotation() - Method in class SuperSmoothMover

    getLane(int) - Method in class VehicleWorld
    Given a y-position, return the lane number (zero-indexed).
    getLaneNumber() - Method in class VehicleSpawner
    Get the lane number (0 indexed) that this VehicleSpawner represents.
    getLaneY(int) - Method in class VehicleWorld
    Given a lane number (zero-indexed), return the y position in the centre of the lane.
    getPreciseRotation() - Method in class SuperSmoothMover
    Get the precise movement rotation of this Actor
    getPreciseX() - Method in class SuperSmoothMover
    Return the exact x-coordinate (as a double).
    getPreciseY() - Method in class SuperSmoothMover
    Return the exact y-coordinate (as a double).
    getRotation() - Method in class SuperSmoothMover
    Get the rotation as an integer.

    An accessor that can be used to get this Vehicle's speed.

    prepareLanes(World, GreenfootImage, VehicleSpawner[], int, int, int, int, boolean, boolean) - Static method in class VehicleWorld
    The prepareLanes method is a static (standalone) method that takes a list of parameters about the desired roadway and then builds it.
    prepareLanes(World, GreenfootImage, VehicleSpawner[], int, int, int, int, boolean, boolean, int) - Static method in class VehicleWorld

    New repel method! Seems to work well.

    setImageRotation(double) - Method in class SuperSmoothMover
    Set the desired angle for the image.

    setLocation(double, double) - Method in class SuperSmoothMover
    Set the location using exact coordinates.
    setLocation(int, int) - Method in class SuperSmoothMover
    Set the location using integer coordinates.
    setMaxVal(int) - Method in class SuperStatBar
    Set the maximum value - for StatBar objects with a single bar only.
    setMaxVal(int[]) - Method in class SuperStatBar
    Set the maximum values for all bars in this StatBar.
    setPrefix(String) - Method in class Counter
    Sets a text prefix that should be displayed before the counter value (e.g.
    setRotation(double) - Method in class SuperSmoothMover
    Set the internal rotation value to a new value.
    setRotation(int) - Method in class SuperSmoothMover
    Set the internal rotation value to a new value.
    setSpeedModifier(double) - Method in class VehicleSpawner
    Do you want a FAST LANE? Or a SLOW LANE? Speed Modifier works as follows: - A speed modifier of 1.0 would have no effect.
    setValue(int) - Method in class Counter
    Set a new counter value.
    show() - Method in class SuperStatBar
    Change the hideAtMax property - make it show the bar even when the primary (0th) stat is at full.
    SHOW_SPAWNERS - Static variable in class VehicleWorld
     
    speed - Variable in class Pedestrian
     
    speed - Variable in class Vehicle
     
    stop() - Method in class Vehicle
     
    SuperSmoothMover - Class in Unnamed Package
    A variation of an actor that maintains a precise location (using doubles for the co-ordinates instead of ints).
    SuperSmoothMover() - Constructor for class SuperSmoothMover
    Default constructor - set staticRotation to false.
    SuperStatBar - Class in Unnamed Package
    New and Improved Stat Bar (Formerly Health Bar).
    SuperStatBar() - Constructor for class SuperStatBar
    Main constructor - A basic constructor that sets default values.
    SuperStatBar(int[], int[], Actor, int, int, int, Color[], Color[], boolean, Color, int) - Constructor for class SuperStatBar
    The king of all StatBar constuctors! Takes details for an array of bars, otherwise the same as above.
    SuperStatBar(int, int, Actor, int, int, int) - Constructor for class SuperStatBar
    A simple constructor for a somewhat customized Green and Red stat bar.
    SuperStatBar(int, int, Actor, int, int, int, Color, Color) - Constructor for class SuperStatBar
    Similar to above, but with the ability to customize colors
    SuperStatBar(int, int, Actor, int, int, int, Color, Color, boolean) - Constructor for class SuperStatBar
    Similar to above, but with the ability to have the bar hide when full - for example if you don't want full health bars shown.
    SuperStatBar(int, int, Actor, int, int, int, Color, Color, boolean, Color, int) - Constructor for class SuperStatBar
    The most detailed constructor! Can specify a border including thickness and color.
    SuperStatBar(int, Actor) - Constructor for class SuperStatBar
    A simple constructor - specify a single value (which will be treated as both current and max for the stat) as well as an owner to follow.

    turn(double) - Method in class SuperSmoothMover
    Turn a specified number of degrees with precision.
    turn(int) - Method in class SuperSmoothMover
    Turn a specified number of degrees.
    turnAngle - Variable in class Vehicle
     
    turnImage(int) - Method in class SuperSmoothMover
    Turn the IMAGE.
    turnTowards(int, int) - Method in class SuperSmoothMover
    Set the internal rotation to face towards a given point.
    turnTowards(Actor) - Method in class SuperSmoothMover
    A short-cut method that I (Jordan Cohen) always thought Greenfoot should have - use the turnTowards method above to face another Actor instead of just a point.
    typeNumber - Variable in class Tower

    Y

    YELLOW_LINE - Static variable in class VehicleWorld
     
    yOffset - Variable in class Vehicle
     

    Z

    zSort(List, World) - Static method in class VehicleWorld
    A z-sort method which will sort Actors so that Actors that are displayed "higher" on the screen (lower y values) will show up underneath Actors that are drawn "lower" on the screen (higher y values), creating a better perspective.
    A B C D E F G H I K L M N O P R S T U V W Y Z
